instructions: Score each candidate set against the query and review_guidance only. Do not assume there is a predefined answer key. Use the visible record names, traits, families, levels, rarity, distances, and excerpts. Penalize results that satisfy a literal token but miss the described task.

top_1_quality.description: 0 irrelevant or harmful; 1 weak; 2 useful; 3 excellent first result.

top_3_quality.description: Overall usefulness of the first three results.

top_10_quality.description: Overall usefulness and coverage of the top ten results.

irrelevant_count.description: Results that do not satisfy the query task.

overbroad_count.description: Generic rules, glossary, hub, or broad reference records where concrete records were desired.

relationship_noise_count.description: Results that appear because they reference or are referenced by the concept but are not themselves good answers.

missing_obvious_result_notes.description: Optional reviewer notes about categories of obvious results that seem absent. Do not invent exact expected records.

aggregate_quality_formula: top_1_quality + top_3_quality + top_10_quality - irrelevant_count - overbroad_count - relationship_noise_count
